QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_23

question.text: ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme) mutual funds have a lock-in period of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELSS has the shortest lock-in among Section 80C investments at just 3 years. It provides 80C tax deduction (under old tax regime) with the potential for equity market returns. **The Trap:** 5 years is the lock-in for tax-saving bank FDs and NSC. 7 years is related to PPF partial withdrawal. ELSS is specifically 3 years. **Cross-Exam Context:** ELSS features are tested in Series V-A (MF), X-A (financial planning), and XVII (retirement products). It offers the dual benefit of tax saving and wealth creation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS can be a good addition to retirement portfolios for young investors — it provides 80C benefit with equity exposure and the shortest lock-in among 80C products.
